rules: [P1] Mandatory EHR retrieval for all patient-specific attributes or demographic calculations prior to inference.
[P1] Map complex clinical narratives into structured formats (e.g., SBAR) for free-text fields.
[P1] Query system metadata to establish current reference dates for temporal logic.
[P1] Apply precise, entity-specific, and temporal filters to retrieval tools to minimize noise.
[P1] Mandate tool execution for factual data retrieval; prohibit direct inference.
[P1] Explicitly evaluate conditional triggers and justify omissions before initiating state-changing actions.
[P1] Verify existence of required data and patient identity via system query before initiating state-changing actions.
[P1] Decompose multi-step workflows into explicit search, evaluation, and execution phases.
[P1] Document the scope of search attempts (e.g., synonyms, resource types, full history) when reporting data absence.
[P1] Perform exhaustive cross-resource verification (e.g., multiple resource types) before confirming data absence.
[P1] Prohibit task termination without executing targeted system queries for required data.
[P1] Separate data retrieval tasks from subsequent conditional action execution to prevent over-automation.
[P1] Verify all mandatory clinical components are present before executing state-changing actions.
[P1] Verify tool execution logs for state-changing actions; do not assume success.
[P1] State reference ranges and timestamps from source documentation when justifying clinical decisions.
[P1] Sort retrieval results by date; filter by timeframe before parsing for the most recent entry.
[P1] Append precise ISO-8601 timestamps and unique patient identifiers to all state-changing clinical entries.
